summary: Install-time credential and crypto-wallet stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded IP
details: The package ships a single file, postinstall.js, wired to both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks (and set as main) in package.json, so it auto-executes on npm install. The script enumerates the user's home, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, /root, and /tmp for wallet and secret files (id.json, wallet.json, keystore.json, metamask.json, phantom.json, seed.txt, mnemonic.txt,.wallet.dat), reads ~/.ssh/ private keys and authorized_keys (id_rsa, id_ecdsa, id_ed25519, deploy_key), ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials,.env files, and.txt/.md/.rtf files whose names or contents match BIP39 seed keywords. It also targets Chrome/Brave/Edge Local Extension Settings for MetaMask (extension id nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn) and Phantom (bfnaelmomeimhlpmgjnjopphhpkkoljpa). Collected contents are POSTed via http.request to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package name mimics a Binance Smart Chain SDK to lure crypto developers, while the tarball contains no SDK code — only the stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
